122852.explanation: It is not an end in itself. The quality of the aldermen and collegial governance is paramount, regardless of gender.

122859.explanation: There is an important task for employers to provide proper housing. This can also be at a location outside the company grounds.

122863.explanation: Costs are also incurred for the collection and processing of bulky waste. Free sounds nice but is paid from the municipality's waste fund, causing even non-polluters to pay.

122865.explanation: This is the entrepreneur’s own task and responsibility. The municipality can raise awareness among entrepreneurs. We do believe the municipality is responsible for making and keeping municipal facilities, sidewalks, and roads accessible for people with disabilities.

122871.explanation: We believe the recent expansion of BOA capacity is sufficient for now. We want to deploy enforcement officers at the right times, including evenings or weekends when there is nuisance. Time should be used efficiently.

122873.explanation: For us, this form of care is important. It can also be provided in a neighboring municipality to offer the best possible care. This could also be in Roerdalen.

122875.explanation: We think a good mix of homes is important, tailored to the needs of our residents. Also semi-detached houses, small homes, and apartments.

122876.explanation: Holiday homes are primarily intended for recreation. We want to maintain this.

122878.explanation: We believe it is important to look out for each other. It makes no sense to push people further into debt. That only causes more problems and thus higher costs in the social domain. Debt forgiveness can be a good solution for a fresh (re)start.

122879.explanation: We believe this is the responsibility of the national government. The municipality should not act as a lackey for the national government.

122881.explanation: We find the locations already designated sufficient. If demand increases, we think it is important that the municipality responds accordingly.

122882.explanation: We think it is important that people receive the right care. Excluding care workers based on their religion, culture, or beliefs is unacceptable. We have the impression this hardly occurs in Roerdalen.

122883.explanation: Price is an important criterion. Quality and continuity of care are just as important. We do believe that financial resources must remain within limits and that there should be sufficient attention to care and support as close by as possible.

122886.explanation: We want to seek expansion of existing industrial areas, fitting within the environment and the green, village character of our municipality.

122891.explanation: We think it is a good idea for the municipality to invest in the outdoor pools in both Montfort and Vlodrop. We greatly appreciate the efforts of the volunteers and believe the municipality should continue to support them well.

122892.explanation: Of course, we find it important that everyone can swim, although we do not primarily see this as a municipal task. Through funds like the Youth Fund, there are opportunities for families with limited means. School swimming takes away school time; development time that children desperately need.

122893.explanation: We believe there should be space and attention for the local language. However, it is not up to the municipality to determine this for schools. We do support that the municipality has worked on Limburg language books for children in daycare and primary school as support.

122898.explanation: We think municipal policy does not need to be adjusted. Investigating alternative locations is a logical step in conversations between initiators and the municipality.

122899.explanation: The municipality is not the competent authority here. However, the municipality has the task of taking citizens’ signals seriously and engaging with them. If waste is not stored properly, the municipality must take action.

122900.explanation: We endorse the importance of peace and nature protection in De Meinweg. However, a general access ban after sunset with intensive enforcement is not feasible and very difficult to implement.

122852.explanation: It is important that men and women are well represented in the municipal executive. That ensures different perspectives. At the same time, it must always be considered who is most suitable for the role. If people are equally suitable, we find a good gender balance important.

122859.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture are responsible for adequate housing of seasonal workers on their own sites. This prevents additional pressure on the housing market and ensures clear responsibility and good supervision.

122863.explanation: Free home collection of bulky waste involves high costs. By bringing bulky waste to the recycling center themselves, we keep costs fair and manageable for all residents.

122865.explanation: CDA Roerdalen wants all residents to participate in society. Financially supporting entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible directly contributes to this.

122871.explanation: We agree with this. In 2026, the number of enforcement officers (BOAs) will already be increased. CDA supports having sufficient BOAs, at least at the level appropriate for a municipality like Roerdalen. The safety and sense of security of residents is very important to us.

122873.explanation: Shelter and support for domestic violence are organized regionally. Facilities are already available in the region. Therefore, it is not necessary to establish a separate women's shelter in Roerdalen.

122875.explanation: Roerdalen builds to meet demand, with the needs of the elderly being an important part. By creating more suitable homes for older people, housing becomes available for families again. Various housing concepts are welcome.

122876.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes can, given the tight housing market, help in some cases. At the same time, Roerdalen is a recreation municipality and holiday homes are important for tourism and our economy. Therefore, a careful consideration is necessary.

122878.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes for low-income residents is important to prevent financial problems. By offering this support, the municipality shows care for residents and prevents problems from worsening, which ultimately leads to higher costs.

122879.explanation: The municipality’s current policy provides support to residents who need it. Increasing the income threshold is therefore not needed; tailored and focused assistance is more important than broadening general limits.

122881.explanation: More charging points for electric cars are needed, but attention must also be paid to sufficient parking spaces for regular cars.

122882.explanation: Where possible, people should be able to choose a care provider that fits their religion, culture, or beliefs. At the same time, there is a great shortage of care providers, so this freedom of choice cannot always be offered.

122883.explanation: When selecting organizations for youth care, price should not be decisive. The quality of care and what is best for the youth must always come first.

122886.explanation: CDA Roerdalen agrees with this. Efforts to expand industrial areas are already underway in Roerdalen. These concern small-scale industrial parks, which increase space for SMEs.

122891.explanation: The Sweeltje swimming pool is important for sports and social meetings in the village. Management and operation have recently been transferred to a volunteer-run foundation. The municipality continues to financially support the pool, making renewal necessary and responsible.

122892.explanation: Providing municipal funds for school swimming is currently not realistic. Both financial resources and available time of teachers and students are urgently needed to teach and reinforce important basic skills in education.

122893.explanation: We have strongly advocated for this. Attention to the Limburg language strengthens culture and identity. A recent research report by the Education Council encourages the use of Limburgish in schools, since it positively affects spelling and reading comprehension of Dutch.

122898.explanation: CDA Roerdalen does not want new solar fields on agricultural land. To achieve sustainability goals, CDA Roerdalen believes that primarily large-scale solar-on-roof solutions should be pursued.

122899.explanation: Actively opposing the storage of asbestos-containing soil waste is unnecessary according to CDA Roerdalen. Strict environmental regulations apply to the storage site, which must be followed.

122900.explanation: A ban on access to De Meinweg after sunset already exists but needs better enforcement. Effective enforcement is necessary to protect nature, peace, and safety.

122852.explanation: Quality must be paramount when forming the municipal executive. This concerns experience, knowledge, and commitment to our municipality. Everyone should have equal opportunities, but a fixed distribution between men and women should never be more important than suitability.

122859.explanation: Employers are responsible for good and safe housing for their workers. Housing must be properly arranged and cause no nuisance to the neighborhood. This ensures clear agreements and a livable environment.

122863.explanation: If bulky waste is collected for free, people are less likely to leave waste on the street or in nature. This keeps our villages clean and tidy. Good waste collection contributes to a safe and livable environment for everyone.

122865.explanation: Accessibility is important. Everyone must be able to participate. But entrepreneurs are responsible for their own buildings. The municipality can advise and think along but cannot fund everything with subsidies.

122871.explanation: More enforcement officers ensure more safety in our villages. Minor offenses and nuisance must be addressed more quickly. Visible street supervision gives residents a sense of security and prevents bigger problems.

122873.explanation: Help for victims of violence is very important, but having a dedicated shelter in Roerdalen is not necessary. This can be better arranged together with other municipalities. This way, the help remains good, safe, and affordable.

122875.explanation: Many elderly people want to continue living in their own village. Therefore, more small, affordable, and lifetime-adapted homes are needed. This allows people to live independently longer, close to family, care, and facilities.

122876.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation and tourism. Allowing them as permanent residences blurs the line between living and recreation and creates more pressure and nuisance. We solve the housing shortage by building regular homes faster and enforcing regulations.

122878.explanation: People with little money already have a hard time. If the municipality stops debt forgiveness, they will get into even more trouble. Therefore, this scheme must remain so everyone can continue to participate.

122879.explanation: Some people earn just too much to qualify for help but still have difficulty making ends meet. By raising the income limit, more residents can receive support. This ensures no one falls through the cracks.

122881.explanation: Sustainability is important. There must be enough charging stations for electric cars. But expansion should match demand and remain affordable for our municipality and residents.

122882.explanation: Care must be good, affordable, and accessible for everyone. The quality of assistance is most important. Choice based on religion or culture should not lead to higher costs or lower quality care.

122883.explanation: Good help for children is more important than just a low price. Children should receive the right care suited to them. The quality of assistance must always come first.

122886.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have room to grow. This creates jobs and a strong local economy. Expansion of industrial sites is possible but must fit the village and not cause nuisance to residents.

122891.explanation: The municipal council decided last year to renovate the swimming pool. Democrats Roerdalen voted in favor of this decision. A good swimming pool is important for sports, health, and meeting places in our municipality.

122892.explanation: All children must learn to swim well. Therefore, in November 2025, Democrats Roerdalen proposed reintroducing school swimming at primary schools. Most parties voted against it. We will keep fighting for school swimming.

122893.explanation: The Limburg dialect belongs to our region and culture. Schools may pay attention to this if they want. It is not mandatory but a nice way to keep our language alive.

122898.explanation: Agricultural land is needed to produce food. That is more important than solar panels or wind turbines on fields. Farmers must be able to continue farming. Therefore, solar parks are not allowed on good agricultural land.

122899.explanation: The health of our residents comes first. Asbestos storage can pose risks to people and the environment. Therefore, the municipality must oppose this and ensure a safe and clean living environment.

122900.explanation: The Meinweg is a vulnerable natural area. In the evening, there is a greater chance of nuisance and damage. If a ban after sunset helps protect nature and safety, the municipality must introduce and strictly enforce it.

122852.explanation: We believe it is important that there should be a proportional distribution because this will improve mutual relations.

122859.explanation: We think this is good to avoid additional strain on the housing market. However, we believe that housing must be good and affordable.

122863.explanation: We believe that free bulky waste collection prevents it from being discarded improperly. It also allows proper recycling.

122865.explanation: This task belongs to the entrepreneurs themselves.

122871.explanation: We have already increased this number, but we may raise it further to improve safety if the situation requires.

122873.explanation: This is arranged regionally by the neighboring municipality, and we want to tackle this together from a central location.

122875.explanation: There is an imbalance in the municipality. This addition will ensure more people move through, freeing up other houses. This will stimulate movement and allow starters to progress.

122876.explanation: We are a tourist municipality. These homes are used for recreational visitors.

122878.explanation: We are here for the residents, and poverty is a major problem. We cannot leave people out in the cold; this would only make problems worse for residents and the municipality.

122879.explanation: Everyone should be able to receive help, regardless of income. We follow national standards.

122881.explanation: We do not yet have enough.

122882.explanation: Care must be accessible to everyone, and attention must be given to care needs and demand.

122883.explanation: We seek good support; price is not the main motivation.

122886.explanation: Companies should expand only on existing industrial sites.

122891.explanation: The swimming pools themselves are responsible, and the municipality provides support in other ways.

122892.explanation: We find it important that everyone can swim. If possible, swimming during school hours is allowed, which is up to the school, but after school must also be possible.

122893.explanation: Schools already have their hands full. This task mainly lies with the parents.

122898.explanation: We are in favor of following the solar ladder.

122899.explanation: We must process it safely, and as long as this happens, we are not opposed. The provincial government is in charge here.

122900.explanation: We believe that vulnerable nature must be protected.

122863.explanation: Offer the possibility to dispose of one cubic meter of bulky waste free of charge three times a year.

122865.explanation: Set clear requirements for new construction. Possibly provide financial support for old buildings.

122875.explanation: Building according to needs promotes movement.

122878.explanation: Structural debt forgiveness increases the tax burden for all other citizens.

122881.explanation: Cover parking spaces with solar panels.

122891.explanation: Transferred to a foundation that has received compensation for this.

122892.explanation: Not feasible. Possibly approach people personally.

122899.explanation: Asbestos must be stored properly and under supervision.

122900.explanation: Except for public roads. Possibility to apply for an exemption, among others for private owners.

statements.title: The municipality must introduce a ban on access to De Meinweg after sunset and enforce it effectively.
Roerdalen must deploy more municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes for the elderly must be built in Roerdalen.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Roerdalen must establish a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must actively oppose the storage of asbestos-containing soil waste at the Montfort landfill.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must enable all primary schools to give attention to the Limburg dialect.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Roerdalen must renovate the Het Sweeltje swimming pool in Montfort.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Business parks in Roerdalen must be allowed to expand.
When creating solar parks, the municipality must first investigate alternative locations before using agricultural land.
